jobs.title: Associate Product Counsel
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Why Harvey

At Harvey, we’re transforming how legal and professional services operate — not incrementally, but end-to-end. By combining frontier agentic AI, an enterprise-grade platform, and deep domain expertise, we’re reshaping how critical knowledge work gets done for decades to come.

This is a rare chance to help build a generational company at a true inflection point. With 1000+ customers in 60+ countries, strong product-market fit, and world-class investor support, we’re scaling fast and defining a new category in real time. The work is ambitious, the bar is high, and the opportunity for growth — personal, professional, and financial — is unmatched.

Our team is sharp, motivated, and deeply committed to the mission. We move fast, operate with intensity, and take real ownership of the problems we tackle — from early thinking to long-term outcomes. We stay close to our customers — from leadership to engineers — and work together to solve real problems with urgency and care. If you thrive in ambiguity, push for excellence, and want to help shape the future of work alongside others who raise the bar, we invite you to build with us.

At Harvey, the future of professional services is being written today — and we’re just getting started.

Role Overview

We are looking for a product attorney to join our legal team to help scale our Product Legal function. You will partner closely with our Engineering, Product, and Design teams, as well as other cross-functional stakeholders, to bring Harvey’s product vision to life.

What You'll Do

Generative AI has the power to greatly change the world and directly impact how things get done. Harvey is at the forefront of this change, and our mission is to make generative AI actionable and useful for our customers. This is a fast moving environment (we are a start-up!) that is serving some of the world’s most renowned law firms and professional organizations. And this role will put you in the center of all of this.

More specifically, you will:

  • Partner cross-functionally to align legal guidance with business goals, including counseling our Product and Engineering teams on key legal issues related to B2B AI systems

  • Research and advise on regulatory frameworks to help design and enhance compliant AI-powered products

  • Partner with marketing teams to align product communications with legal and regulatory standards to support brand credibility and trust

  • Support key initiatives to further develop and mature the legal team’s capabilities and impact

  • Build communication styles that resonate with your teams to balance risk-taking with responsible development in a fast-moving environment

  • Have a strong sense of ownership, curiosity, and can demonstrate sound judgment in ambiguous situations.

What You Have

  • A law degree from an accredited law school

  • Admitted to practice law and in good standing in at least one jurisdiction and able to practice in California

  • 2-3 years of law firm and/or in-house experience

  • Demonstrated knowledge of laws and regulations relating to several of: artificial intelligence, intellectual property, privacy, product liability, marketing, and/or consumer protection

  • Experience advising clients on AI or technology-related legal matters in a product legal or tech transactions capacity

  • Ability to think creatively, function independently and work proactively in ambiguous situations without clear precedents

  • Team-focused and collaborative working style and willingness to build expertise to solve new challenges in an evolving practice

  • Hyper-responsive and service-oriented attitude

  • A love for solving hard problems in a fast-paced environment

Compensation

$180,000-$220,000 USD

Depending on your location, an Applicant Privacy Notice may apply to you. You can find all of our Applicant Privacy Notices [here].

jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer, Full Stack - New Verticals
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Why Harvey

At Harvey, we’re transforming how legal and professional services operate — not incrementally, but end-to-end. By combining frontier agentic AI, an enterprise-grade platform, and deep domain expertise, we’re reshaping how critical knowledge work gets done for decades to come.

This is a rare chance to help build a generational company at a true inflection point. With 1000+ customers in 60+ countries, strong product-market fit, and world-class investor support, we’re scaling fast and defining a new category in real time. The work is ambitious, the bar is high, and the opportunity for growth — personal, professional, and financial — is unmatched.

Our team is sharp, motivated, and deeply committed to the mission. We move fast, operate with intensity, and take real ownership of the problems we tackle — from early thinking to long-term outcomes. We stay close to our customers — from leadership to engineers — and work together to solve real problems with urgency and care. If you thrive in ambiguity, push for excellence, and want to help shape the future of work alongside others who raise the bar, we invite you to build with us.

At Harvey, the future of professional services is being written today — and we’re just getting started.

Role Overview

We’re hiring a Senior Software Engineer, New Verticals to build new products and capabilities for the legal industry from first principles.

This is a deeply technical, end-to-end engineering role for someone who is excited to own ambiguous, high-leverage problems across the full stack. You will work on greenfield and early-stage product areas that span user-facing applications, backend services, data systems, document and knowledge pipelines, and agentic workflows. Depending on the problem, that may mean building production frontend experiences, designing APIs and services, modeling data, standing up ETL pipelines, improving retrieval and evaluation loops, orchestrating long-running agents, or shipping platform capabilities that make entirely new solution categories possible.

You will partner closely with customers, product, design, and other engineers to understand complex legal workflows and translate them into robust, scalable software. The goal is not just to deliver custom solutions, but to identify repeatable patterns and turn them into durable product and platform capabilities that can define new verticals for Harvey.

This role is hybrid, based in New York, NY and offers relocation assistance.

How You Will Make a 10x Impact

High Beta: You understand that ambiguity and volatility are part of building new products. You can move from undefined problem spaces to concrete technical plans, test assumptions quickly, and adjust without attachment when the evidence changes.

Customer-Driven Engineering: You ground technical decisions in real user problems. You are excited to work directly with customers and internal partners, understand complex workflows in detail, and build systems that solve practical, high-value problems in production.

Relentless Builder Mentality: You have a strong bias toward shipping. You know how to balance speed and rigor, and you are comfortable building the first version, learning from usage, and then improving architecture, reliability, and scale over time.

Strong Technical Judgment: You can navigate tradeoffs across product, infrastructure, and data. You know when to invest in abstractions, when to keep things simple, and how to make systems that are both pragmatic in the short term and durable in the long term.

What You'll Do

  • Design and build production systems end-to-end across frontend applications, backend services, APIs, data models, and developer tooling.

  • Develop new vertical product capabilities involving document ingestion, workflow orchestration, retrieval, structured outputs, agent frameworks, and human-in-the-loop review flows.

  • Build and operate data-intensive systems, including ETL pipelines, analytical data models, serving layers, and interfaces for querying and exploring large datasets.

  • Work across a modern technical stack that may include Python, TypeScript, SQL, cloud infrastructure, agent orchestration engines, data processing platforms, and analytical databases.

  • Define service boundaries, schema design, and system architecture for new product areas with an emphasis on reliability, extensibility, and speed of iteration.

  • Ship customer-facing features quickly, then harden them for scale through observability, testing, performance tuning, and operational excellence.

  • Partner directly with customers and internal stakeholders to understand domain-specific workflows, map requirements into technical designs, and iterate rapidly on real usage.

  • Build evaluation, monitoring, and feedback systems that measure product quality in production and inform engineering and model decisions.

  • Contribute reusable platform patterns, abstractions, and internal tools so successful solutions can scale across customers and verticals.

  • Help drive technical direction in ambiguous spaces, making strong engineering decisions with incomplete information and a high degree of ownership.

What You Have

  • 8+ years of experience building and operating production software, with meaningful ownership of systems from initial concept through launch and iteration.

  • Strong engineering fundamentals across backend systems, APIs, data modeling, and exposure to at least one modern frontend stack.

  • Experience building full-stack products, not just isolated services or UI layers.

  • Experience working with data pipelines, analytical systems, or document-heavy workflows, including schema design, ETL, query performance, and data reliability.

  • Experience building AI-powered or workflow-driven applications, such as retrieval systems, agentic workflows, orchestration layers, evaluation pipelines, or structured generation systems.

  • Comfort working across a broad technical surface area, including application code, infrastructure, databases, observability, and internal tooling.

  • Ability to debug and improve complex production systems using logs, metrics, traces, dashboards, and strong engineering intuition.

  • Strong product sense and the ability to translate ambiguous business or user needs into thoughtful technical designs.

  • Comfort partnering directly with customers and cross-functional teams to scope problems, validate solutions, and drive adoption.

  • Clear, concise communication, a low-ego working style, and enthusiasm for collaborating in person with teammates in New York.

Compensation Range

$260,000-$310,000 USD

#LI-SB1

Harvey is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition, or any other basis protected by law.

We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities, and requests can be made by emailing accommodations@harvey.ai

jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Frontend
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Why Harvey

At Harvey, we’re transforming how legal and professional services operate — not incrementally, but end-to-end. By combining frontier agentic AI, an enterprise-grade platform, and deep domain expertise, we’re reshaping how critical knowledge work gets done for decades to come.

This is a rare chance to help build a generational company at a true inflection point. With 1000+ customers in 60+ countries, strong product-market fit, and world-class investor support, we’re scaling fast and defining a new category in real time. The work is ambitious, the bar is high, and the opportunity for growth — personal, professional, and financial — is unmatched.

Our team is sharp, motivated, and deeply committed to the mission. We move fast, operate with intensity, and take real ownership of the problems we tackle — from early thinking to long-term outcomes. We stay close to our customers — from leadership to engineers — and work together to solve real problems with urgency and care. If you thrive in ambiguity, push for excellence, and want to help shape the future of work alongside others who raise the bar, we invite you to build with us.

At Harvey, the future of professional services is being written today — and we’re just getting started.

Role Overview

As a Senior Software Engineer, Frontend at Harvey, you will own and lead engineering projects that define the user experience of our different products: Assistant, Vault, Workflows, and Harvey in Microsoft Word and Outlook. You will also lead frontend engineering on new products that we are building at Harvey. The team is composed of highly motivated, skilled, and considerate engineers. Everything we touch is polished to high standards extending to all aspects of our work –  from design to architecture to code to slack communication. If any of this resonates with you, this role might be right for you.

This role is based in San Francisco, CA. We use a hybrid in-person work model and offer relocation assistance to new employees.

What You'll Do

  • Build new interfaces for lawyers and organizations to collaborate with AI.

  • Lead end-to-end UI features for Assistant, Vault, Workflows, our Word/Outlook add-ins, and future Harvey products — from scoping and design reviews to implementation, launch, and iteration.

  • Build polished, high-performance React + TypeScript interfaces using TailwindCSS, with crisp interactions, resilient state management, and strong error handling.

  • Architect reusable components and patterns and evolve our internal design system.

  • Optimize frontend performance at scale.

  • Collaborate tightly with design and backend/infrastructure to shape APIs and UX for long-running jobs, streaming results, and tool-calling workflows.

  • Raise the bar on frontend reliability and security UX for enterprise and regulated environments.

  • Mentor peers through thoughtful code reviews and documentation; help refine our frontend standards and developer experience.

What You Have

  • Experience with React, Typescript, and CSS (we use Tailwind CSS)

  • Track record of building fast-growing SaaS products by leveraging PWA technologies

  • Track record of shipping highly intuitive products, strong attention to detail

  • Experience working at startups and scaleups (anywhere from early-to-late-stage) is a plus

Compensation Range

$200,000 - $260,000 USD

Depending on your location, an Applicant Privacy Notice may apply to you. You can find all of our Applicant Privacy Notices [here].

We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities, and requests can be made by emailing accommodations@harvey.ai

WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build new interfaces for lawyers and organizations to collaborate with AI.

 - Lead end-to-end UI features for Assistant, Vault, Workflows, our Word/Outlook add-ins, and future Harvey products — from scoping and design reviews to implementation, launch, and iteration.

 - Build polished, high-performance React + TypeScript interfaces using TailwindCSS, with crisp interactions, resilient state management, and strong error handling.

 - Architect reusable components and patterns and evolve our internal design system.

 - Optimize frontend performance at scale.

 - Collaborate tightly with design and backend/infrastructure to shape APIs and UX for long-running jobs, streaming results, and tool-calling workflows.

 - Raise the bar on frontend reliability and security UX for enterprise and regulated environments.

 - Mentor peers through thoughtful code reviews and documentation; help refine our frontend standards and developer experience.


WHAT YOU HAVE

 - Experience with React, Typescript, and CSS (we use TailwindCSS)

 - Track record of building fast-growing SaaS products by leveraging PWA technologies

 - Track record of shipping highly intuitive products, strong attention to detail

 - Experience working at startups and scaleups (anywhere from early-to-late-stage) is a plus


COMPENSATION RANGE

$238,000 - $290,000 USD


DEPENDING ON YOUR LOCATION, AN APPLICANT PRIVACY NOTICE MAY APPLY TO YOU. YOU CAN FIND ALL OF OUR APPLICANT PRIVACY NOTICES [HERE https://www.notion.so/harveyai/Harvey-Candidate-Privacy-Policies-319ac3fcdd7a803bb807d5094f249922].

Harvey is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition, or any other basis protected by law.

We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities, and requests can be made by emailing accommodations@harvey.ai

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, Billing
jobs.descriptionHtml:
Why Harvey

At Harvey, we’re transforming how legal and professional services operate — not incrementally, but end-to-end. By combining frontier agentic AI, an enterprise-grade platform, and deep domain expertise, we’re reshaping how critical knowledge work gets done for decades to come.

This is a rare chance to help build a generational company at a true inflection point. With 1000+ customers in 60+ countries, strong product-market fit, and world-class investor support, we’re scaling fast and defining a new category in real time. The work is ambitious, the bar is high, and the opportunity for growth — personal, professional, and financial — is unmatched.

Our team is sharp, motivated, and deeply committed to the mission. We move fast, operate with intensity, and take real ownership of the problems we tackle — from early thinking to long-term outcomes. We stay close to our customers — from leadership to engineers — and work together to solve real problems with urgency and care. If you thrive in ambiguity, push for excellence, and want to help shape the future of work alongside others who raise the bar, we invite you to build with us.

At Harvey, the future of professional services is being written today — and we’re just getting started.

Role Overview

Harvey is looking for a Senior Product Manager, Billing to build and scale the systems that support how enterprise customers understand, trust, and pay for their activity across Harvey’s AI platform. This role sits within the product organization and partners closely with engineering, design, GTM, finance, and legal to bring reliable billing capabilities to market. You will own a critical part of Harvey’s commercial infrastructure. This is a high-impact role for a product leader who can operate at both the customer experience layer and the underlying platform layer as Harvey’s pricing and packaging continue to evolve.

What You'll Do

  • Drive the vision, strategy, and execution for Harvey’s billing infrastructure.

  • Partner with engineering and design to build billing capabilities that are accurate, auditable, flexible, and intuitive for enterprise customers.

  • Collaborate with go-to-market, finance, legal, sales, and customer success teams to support complex enterprise contracts.

  • Shape the customer-facing billing experience, including invoices, reporting, and account-level transparency, while ensuring the underlying infrastructure can scale.

  • Help Harvey scale a trusted commercial platform that meets the needs of enterprise customers, internal operators, and financial reporting stakeholders.

What You Have

  • 5+ years of product management experience, with meaningful time spent on billing, platform, or infrastructure.

  • Experience building billing, ideally in a SaaS, enterprise software, or AI platform environment.

  • Familiarity with third-party billing platforms.

  • Strong understanding of the billing stack, including event capture, aggregation, invoicing, revenue recognition, accounting handoffs, and operational controls.

  • Excellent stakeholder management skills, with the ability to balance customer needs, commercial priorities, finance requirements, legal considerations, and engineering tradeoffs.

  • A systems-thinking approach to product development, with the ability to design architectures and workflows that are reliable, transparent, auditable, and adaptable.

Compensation

$175,000 - $265,000 USD

Depending on your location, an Applicant Privacy Notice may apply to you. You can find all of our Applicant Privacy Notices [here].

#LI-SB1

Harvey is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin, disability, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy or related condition, or any other basis protected by law.

We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities, and requests can be made by emailing accommodations@harvey.ai
